disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A sudden eruption of round target/'bullseye' spots that began on the backs of both hands and wrists about 24 hours ago and has spread symmetrically onto the chest, feeling hot and burning.
trigger_question_examples: Where did the rash start and where is it now?
How quickly did it come up and spread?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started on the backs of my hands and wrists and has spread up onto my chest over about a day. They're round bullseye spots and the skin feels hot and burning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The individual lesions are classic three-zone target/iris lesions — a dark dusky (sometimes blistered) centre, a surrounding pale oedematous ring, and an outer red halo.
trigger_question_examples: What do the individual spots look like up close?
Do the spots have rings or different colours in them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Each spot has rings — a dark, dusky, slightly blistered middle, then a pale puffy ring, then a red ring around the outside. They look just like little targets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A cold sore (herpes labialis) on the lip about 10 days before the rash, now crusting/healing — the preceding trigger.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any cold sores, infections or illnesses in the last couple of weeks?
Anything on your lips or face before the rash started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I had a cold sore on my lip about 10 days ago and it's healing over now. I get them from time to time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new medicines, antibiotics, anti-inflammatories or other tablets started in the recent weeks — arguing against a drug-triggered eruption.
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medicines, antibiotics or painkillers recently?
Any new tablets, supplements or creams in the last few weeks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't started anything new — no new tablets, antibiotics or painkillers. It just came up after the cold sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sores, ulcers, erosions or blistering in the mouth, eyes or genitals, no skin peeling or sloughing in sheets, no severe skin pain and no high fever — arguing strongly against a severe mucocutaneous reaction (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / TEN).
trigger_question_examples: Any sores, ulcers or blistering in your mouth, eyes or genitals?
Is the skin peeling off in sheets anywhere, and do you have a high fever or feel severely unwell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sores or ulcers in my mouth, eyes or private area, and the skin isn't peeling off in sheets. I don't have a high fever and the spots aren't very painful — I just feel a bit run down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No swelling of the face, lips, tongue or throat, no difficulty breathing, no widespread hives and no faintness — no features of an evolving systemic allergic reaction / anaphylaxis or airway angioedema.
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling of your face, lips, tongue or throat, or any trouble breathing?
Any widespread hives, wheeze or feeling faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my face, lips and throat are fine, I'm breathing normally, there are no hives all over and I don't feel faint. It's the target spots on my arms and chest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked patient anxiety about the appearance and rapid spread of the eruption, with the itch/burning far outweighing any true pain.
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling about the rash?
Is it more burning/itchy or actually painful?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm scared because it's spreading so fast, but the spots are more hot and prickly than painful.
